pi_items.source: Verified in setup_task.sh line 20 where PROJECT_DIR is set, and export_result.sh line 7 where it is used. This is a standard Android Studio projects directory following the convention /home/{user}/AndroidStudioProjects/{ProjectName}.
Verified in export_result.sh line 14 where LAYOUT_FILE is defined and used to check for widget layout file existence. This path follows standard Android resource structure for layout XML files.

Verified in export_result.sh line 15 where INFO_FILE is defined. This path follows the Android convention for AppWidget provider info XML files located in res/xml/ directory. The verifier.py line 84 specifically parses this XML with ET.fromstring().

Verified in export_result.sh line 16 where PROVIDER_FILE is defined as the Kotlin class file path. Follows standard Android package structure with package name com.example.weatherapp. The verifier.py line 113-119 checks this file for AppWidgetProvider class and required methods.
Verified in export_result.sh line 17 and multiple references in setup_task.sh for manifest creation and modification tracking. This is the standard Android application manifest file location.

Verified in task.json metadata line 22 and confirmed in verifier.py line 37 which uses these as default values. Lines 63-65 of verifier.py specifically search for these exact ID strings in the layout XML android:id attributes. These are the three TextViews required for the weather widget display.

Verified in setup_task.sh lines 30, 33 where the SunflowerApp package com.google.samples.apps.sunflower is explicitly renamed to com.example.weatherapp using sed. Also verified in verifier.py line 135 where the manifest is checked for 'WeatherWidgetProvider' which would be in this package. The AppWidget gradle plugin and Android Studio expect the provider class to be in this package namespace.

privileged_info_summary: The task 'add_app_widget@1' requires an agent to add an Android App Widget to a WeatherApp project. The project is created at /home/ga/AndroidStudioProjects/WeatherApp and uses package name com.example.weatherapp. The agent must create four components: (1) A widget layout file at app/src/main/res/layout/widget_weather.xml containing three TextViews with IDs widget_city_name, widget_temperature, and widget_condition; (2) A widget metadata configuration file at app/src/main/res/xml/weather_widget_info.xml containing an appwidget-provider element with attributes minWidth=180dp, minHeight=60dp, updatePeriodMillis=1800000 (30 minutes, the Android minimum), and reference to the layout; (3) A Kotlin provider class WeatherWidgetProvider.kt extending AppWidgetProvider with onUpdate method implementation using RemoteViews; (4) Registration in AndroidManifest.xml with a receiver element, APPWIDGET_UPDATE intent action, and appwidget.provider metadata tag. The project must build successfully via Gradle assembleDebug. The task data is synthetic, created from a template (SunflowerApp) and specialized for this widget implementation exercise.
